mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held at the zero lower bound compresses the risk-free discount rate and induces portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points from its trailing 3-month high signals accelerating labor market repair, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2pp from its 3-month trailing high
failure_condition: If post-trigger 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 45% of the time or the average return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in real retail sales and industrial production confirms a broad demand expansion that outstrips supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either series alone.
failure_condition: If the bivariate interaction term fails to significantly improve return prediction over univariate RSAFS (F-test p > 0.05) or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is contained below 2.5% year-over-year, accommodative monetary policy represented by a low federal funds rate combined with rising nonfarm payrolls drives positive 20-day S&P 500 returns through real income growth and low discount rates.
failure_condition: If during triggered low-inflation months the 20-day forward return is negative more than 45% of the time or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.3 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects increasing manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If months with above-median INDPRO momentum produce non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding their 12-month trailing average by more than 5% signal a durable recovery in residential investment and construction employment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds trailing 12-month average by more than 5%
failure_condition: If triggered months produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences or the average return is below zero over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in retail sales and payroll employment captures income-supported consumption growth that is more sustainable than credit-driven demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the product of RSAFS and PAYEMS momentum ranks fails to achieve a rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment during a high-unemployment regime where the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5% signals deteriorating aggregate income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change is negative and UNRATE exceeds 6.5%
failure_condition: If triggered months produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not negative (t-test p > 0.1) over a 3-year window.
